page: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage capabilities include 7559 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU hardware is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational and memory performance. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top 10% tier. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25% tier. The memory subsystem is highly efficient, with PassMark memory scores and database operations placing in the top 10% tier, supported by a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 6246.85 GB/sec. For database workloads, it achieves 93.4 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Large-model LLM inference represents a relative performance tradeoff, dropping to the middle 50% tier for 70B parameter models, whereas small-model prompt processing reaches the top 10% tier at 5006.15 tokens per second.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity, this server offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and data-heavy enterprise workloads. The combination of 512.0 GB of RAM and 7559 GB of local disk space makes it highly suitable for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and data warehousing. It is also well-suited for high-throughput web serving, heavy decompression tasks, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores, 32 vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, with PassMark database operations and memory scores ranking in the top 10%. It achieves strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, though large-model LLM inference performance is moderate. With 16.0 GB of memory per core and substantial local storage, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy and storage-intensive workloads. It is optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, massive local storage, and top-tier database processing performance.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable
Top-tier performance in database operations, memory benchmarks, and single-core tasks
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving
